name: Trip aggregator background worker
description: Background worker that computes trip summary rows from a route's segments and GPS track once the route looks finalized (no new segments in the last N minutes OR all expected segment files uploaded). Reads GPS geometry from the routes.geometry column (PostGIS LineString) for distance/max-speed/avg-speed estimates; uses segment timestamps for duration. Calls the reverse-geocode client for start_address/end_address. Writes into the trips table via UpsertTrip so re-runs are idempotent.
acceptance_criteria: internal/worker/trip_aggregator.go defines a TripAggregator struct with Queries (db), Geocoder (*geocode.Client), and config (PollInterval, FinalizedAfter time.Duration)
TripAggregator.Run(ctx context.Context) loops: find routes without a trip row (or with stale computed_at before the latest segment), aggregate, UpsertTrip, sleep PollInterval

Aggregation computes: distance_meters from ST_Length on the geography cast of geometry; duration_seconds from end_time - start_time; max_speed_mps + avg_speed_mps by segment-pair haversine / dt (engagement metric can be 0 for now, filled in by event-detector-worker later)
For a route missing a geometry, the aggregator still writes a trip row with NULLed distance/speed fields so the UI can show 'no GPS' rather than missing entirely
cmd/server/main.go wires up the aggregator (starts in a goroutine with the server context) behind a TRIP_AGGREGATOR_ENABLED env var (defaults true)

internal/worker/trip_aggregator_test.go uses a real Postgres (matches the pattern existing tests use; skip with t.Skip if DATABASE_URL is unset) to verify: fresh route computes a trip, re-running is idempotent, missing geometry leaves stats NULL
go vet ./... and go build ./... pass cleanly
